title: Electronic Bill Payments System
body_text:
Electronic Bill Payments System
1	Introduction to the Electronic Bill Payments System
1.1	Overview of the Electronic Bill Payments System
Central 1芒聙聶s Electronic Bill Payments System allows your financial institution to submit customer-initiated bill payments to Central 1 for further distribution to participating billers.
Customers enroll for bill payment services through your financial institution and designate which billers they want to pay. Electronic bill payments can be made via numerous channels, including online banking, IVR, or ATMs (depending on which options your financial institution offers). You are responsible for marketing the service and enrolling their customers.
Thousands of billers are currently set up on Central 1芒聙聶s Electronic Bill Payment System. However, you can promote the service to new billers and can help enroll them on the system. Billers that choose to participate in Central 1芒聙聶s Electronic Bill Payments System must sign an agreement with Central 1. Central 1 provides each biller with detailed transaction information and credits the biller according to the method agreed upon during enrollment.
Your financial institution can send bill payment transactions to Central 1 via file transmission or via direct connection to Central 1芒聙聶s eCommSwitch. Bill payments submitted via file transmission are sent as batch files for financial institutions in Ontario. For financial institutions that establish a direct connection to Central 1芒聙聶s eCommSwitch, bill payments can be sent to Central聽1 as they are made (real-time), or they can be sent at a specified time when the financial institution opens its gateway.
Central 1 can hold your financial institution芒聙聶s bill payments database. Alternatively, you or your banking service provider can create and manage the bill payments database. However, financial institutions that send batch files from the Ontario region must hold their own database.
Bill payment transactions are validated to ensure that they follow the billers芒聙聶 verification criteria. If your financial institution has a direct connection to Central 1芒聙聶s Electronic Bill Payments System, the validation process occurs when your customer sets up a biller account. If your financial institution sends bill payments to Central 1 via file transmission, the transactions may or may not be validated before they are sent to Central 1. Regardless of how bill payments are sent to Central 1, they are all validated when they are received at Central 1. Transactions that fail Central 1芒聙聶s validation process are rejected, and your financial institution is advised via a Central 1 report.
Central 1 provides electronic bill payment reports you can download from Central 1芒聙聶s File and Report Exchange. See the File Exchange and Report Distribution Part for information on accessing the File and Report Exchange.
Central 1 debits your financial institution芒聙聶s Central 1 account for bill payment settlement. If you don芒聙聶t have an account with Central 1, settlement is provided via AFT.
Your financial institution must reconcile its electronic bill payments daily and investigate if a difference is found between the amount reported by Central 1 and the amount reported by your banking system.
Most bill payment traces are sent and received via the Online Tracing System (OLT).
Related Payment Solutions Manual Parts
File Exchange and Report Distribution Part
Online Tracing System Part
1.2	Financial Institution Transaction Limit
Your financial institution has a transaction limit that specifies the maximum amount of each bill payment. This limit applies globally to all your billers. However, for Central 1芒聙聶s real-time clients, this global change doesn芒聙聶t affect any unique biller limits you may have established (maximum of ten billers). See Section 1.3, Biller Transaction Limits, for more information.
To request a change to your financial institution芒聙聶s global electronic bill payment transaction limit, complete the Financial Institution Transaction Limit Modification Request 芒聙聯 Electronic Bill Payments System, Form 2424. See Figure 1. Submit the form as directed on page 2 of the form.
Figure 1: Financial Institution Transaction Limit Modification Request 芒聙聯 Electronic Bill Payments System, Form 2424
Changes to your financial institution芒聙聶s transaction limit is subject to service charges. Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario can refer to the Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule, Form 2725.
Related Information
Financial Institution Transaction Limit Modification Request 芒聙聯 Electronic Bill Payments System,
Form 2424
Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule,
Form 2725
(available to Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario only)
1.3	Biller Transaction Limits
The biller transaction limit service allows your financial institution to set a transaction limit on a certain number of billers. This service is only available to Central 1芒聙聶s real-time clients. It isn芒聙聶t compatible for financial institutions that use batch integration, since the biller transaction limit is only verified for electronic bill payments made online, by telephone banking, at an ATM, or in-branch.
Upon request, Central 1 will configure the transaction limits for up to ten billers. You can work with your banking service provider to determine which billers to restrict to a lower dollar amount or a higher dollar amount, or which to filter out of your available billers.
The biller transaction limit always takes precedence over your financial institution芒聙聶s transaction limit. When considering a change to biller transaction limits, you can choose from the following options:
Reduce payment amounts to high-risk billers
芒聙聯 Set up a transaction limit for high-risk billers (e.g., ABC Fitness) that is lower than your financial institution limit. The lower limit is per transaction, so multiple payments that are less than the limit could still be made to the same biller. However, multiple transactions of the same dollar amount and on the same date are handled as duplicate payments when Central 1芒聙聶s bill payment system processes the transactions. You can identify duplicate payments on your bill payment reports.
Increase payment amounts for low-risk billers
芒聙聯 Set up a biller transaction limit for low-risk billers (e.g., CRA) that is higher than your financial institution limit. The increased limit allows your customers to make higher value payments to that biller.
Revoke payments to a biller
芒聙聯 Set up a minimal amount (e.g., $0.01) to block your customers from making electronic bill payments to specific billers (e.g., prepaid cards); or, if your financial institution manages its own biller list (e.g., with a banking system provider), you can simply remove specific billers from your list.
To request or modify a biller transaction limit, complete the Biller Transaction Limit Modification Request 芒聙聯 Electronic Bill Payments System, Form 9116. See Figure 2. Submit the form as directed on page 2 of the form.
Figure 2: Biller Transaction Limit Modification Request 芒聙聯 Electronic Bill Payments System, Form 9116
Biller transaction limits are subject to service charges. Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario can refer to the Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule, Form 2725.
Related Information
Biller Transaction Limit Modification Request 芒聙聯 Electronic Bill Payments System,
Form 9116
Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule,
Form 2725
(available to Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario only)
1.4	Bill Payment Cut-Off Times and Billers with Non-Standard Pricing
Regardless of the submission method used to submit a bill payment, bill payments are subject to cut-off times. Generally, bill payments are forwarded to the biller芒聙聶s financial institution on the business day after they are made if they are submitted to Central 1 by a specific deadline. Payments submitted after the deadline are forwarded to the biller芒聙聶s financial institution one business day later than those submitted before the deadline. However, cut-off times vary based on the bill payment芒聙聶s submission method and/or biller specific deadlines.
Submission method cut-off times:
Submission of electronic bill payments via file transmission
11:00 pm PT / 2:00 am ET
Submission of bill payments via direct connection to Central 1芒聙聶s eCommSwitch
11:59 pm PT / 2:59 am ET
Biller-specific cut-off times:
Canada Revenue Agency (CRA)
芒聢聮
10:30 am PT / 1:30 pm ET (bill payments received before this time are processed on the same day)
芒聢聮
11:59 pm PT / 2:59 am ET (bill payments received after 10:30 am PT / 1:30 pm ET and before this time are processed the next business day)
Hydro Quebec / Revenu Quebec
3:00 pm PT / 6:00 pm ET
Vancity VISA, Vancity Com. Inv. Bank VISA, Community VISA
3:00 pm PT / 6:00 pm ET
Note:	Biller-specific cut-off times are subject to change and are updated on a best-efforts basis.
Additionally, a small number of billers are subject to non-standard pricing. See Billers with Non-Standard Pricing, Form 10183, for more information. Pricing will differ based on what class member you are designated as. Reach out to your Account Executive for more information.
